children.heading: Authority of Secretary
children.label: a Authority of Secretary
children.text: Beginning in fiscal year 2005 and thereafter, the Secretary may establish, modify, charge, and collect recreation fees at Federal recreational lands and waters as provided for in this section.
The amount of the recreation fee shall be commensurate with the benefits and services provided to the visitor.
The Secretary shall consider the aggregate effect of recreation fees on recreation users and recreation service providers.
The Secretary shall consider comparable fees charged elsewhere and by other public agencies and by nearby private sector operators.
The Secretary shall consider the public policy or management objectives served by the recreation fee.
The Secretary shall obtain input from the appropriate Recreation Resource Advisory Committee, as provided in .section 6803(d) of this title
The Secretary shall consider such other factors or criteria as determined appropriate by the Secretary.

children.heading: Special considerations
children.label: c Special considerations
children.text: The Secretary shall establish the minimum number of recreation fees and shall avoid the collection of multiple or layered recreation fees for similar uses, activities, or programs.

children.heading: Prohibition on fees for certain activities or services
children.label: 1 Prohibition on fees for certain activities or services
A
children.text: Solely for parking, undesignated parking, or picnicking along roads or trailsides.

children.label: B
children.text: For general access unless specifically authorized under this section.

children.label: C
children.text: For dispersed areas with low or no investment unless specifically authorized under this section.

children.label: D
children.text: For persons who are driving through, walking through, boating through, horseback riding through, or hiking through Federal recreational lands and waters without using the facilities and services.

children.label: E
children.text: For camping at undeveloped sites that do not provide a minimum number of facilities and services as described in subsection (g)(2)(A).

children.label: F
children.text: For use of overlooks or scenic pullouts.

children.label: G
children.text: For travel by private, noncommercial vehicle over any national parkway or any road or highway established as a part of the Federal-aid System, as defined in , which is commonly used by the public as a means of travel between two places either or both of which are outside any unit or area at which recreation fees are charged under this chapter.section 101 of title 231See References in Text note below.1

children.label: H
children.text: For travel by private, noncommercial vehicle, boat, or aircraft over any road or highway, waterway, or airway to any land in which such person has any property right if such land is within any unit or area at which recreation fees are charged under this chapter.

children.label: I
children.text: For any person who has a right of access for hunting or fishing privileges under a specific provision of law or treaty.

children.label: J
children.text: For any person who is engaged in the conduct of official Federal, State, Tribal, or local government business.

children.label: K
children.text: For special attention or extra services necessary to meet the needs of the disabled.

children.heading: Relation to fees for use of highways or roads
children.label: 2 Relation to fees for use of highways or roads
children.text: An entity that pays a special recreation permit fee or similar permit fee shall not be subject to a road cost-sharing fee or a fee for the use of highways or roads that are open to private, noncommercial use within the boundaries of any Federal recreational lands or waters, as authorized under .section 537 of this title

children.heading: Prohibition on fees for certain persons or places
children.label: 3 Prohibition on fees for certain persons or places
A
children.text: Any person under 16 years of age.

children.label: B
children.text: Outings conducted for noncommercial educational purposes by schools or bona fide academic institutions.

children.label: C
children.text: The U.S.S. Arizona Memorial, Independence National Historical Park, any unit of the National Park System within the District of Columbia, or Arlington House-Robert E. Lee National Memorial.

children.label: D
children.text: The Flight 93 National Memorial.

children.label: E
children.text: Entrance on other routes into the Great Smoky Mountains National Park or any part thereof unless fees are charged for entrance into that park on main highways and thoroughfares.

children.label: F
children.text: Entrance on units of the National Park System containing deed restrictions on charging fees.

children.label: G
children.text: An area or unit covered under section 203 of the Alaska National Interest Lands Conservation Act (; ), with the exception of Denali National Park and Preserve.Public Law 96–48716 U.S.C. 410hh–2

children.label: H
children.text: A unit of the National Wildlife Refuge System created, expanded, or modified by the Alaska National Interest Lands Conservation Act ().Public Law 96–487

children.label: I
children.text: Any person who visits a unit or area under the jurisdiction of the United States Fish and Wildlife Service and who has been issued a valid migratory bird hunting and conservation stamp issued under .section 718b of this title

children.label: J
children.text: Any person engaged in a nonrecreational activity authorized under a valid permit issued under any other Act, including a valid grazing permit.

children.heading: No restriction on recreation opportunities
children.label: 4 No restriction on recreation opportunities
children.text: Nothing in this chapter shall limit the use of recreation opportunities only to areas designated for collection of recreation fees.

children.heading: Authorized sites for entrance fees
children.label: 1 Authorized sites for entrance fees
children.text: The Secretary of the Interior may charge an entrance fee for a unit of the National Park System, including a national monument administered by the National Park Service, or for a unit of the National Wildlife Refuge System.

children.heading: Prohibited sites
children.label: 2 Prohibited sites
children.text: The Secretary shall not charge an entrance fee for Federal recreational lands and waters managed by the Bureau of Land Management, the Bureau of Reclamation, or the Forest Service.
A National Conservation Area.
A National Volcanic Monument.
A destination visitor or interpretive center that provides a broad range of interpretive services, programs, and media.

children.label: A
children.text: that provides significant opportunities for outdoor recreation;

children.label: B
children.text: that has substantial Federal investments;

children.label: C
children.text: where fees can be efficiently collected; and

children.label: i
children.text: Designated developed parking.

children.label: ii
children.text: A permanent toilet facility.

children.label: iii
children.text: A permanent trash receptacle.

children.label: iv
children.text: Interpretive sign, exhibit, or kiosk.

children.label: v
children.text: Picnic tables.

children.label: vi
children.text: Security services.

children.heading: NPS and USFWS authority
children.label: 1 NPS and USFWS authority
children.text: Except as limited by subsection (d), the Secretary of the Interior may charge an expanded amenity recreation fee, either in addition to an entrance fee or by itself, at Federal recreational lands and waters under the jurisdiction of the National Park Service or the United States Fish and Wildlife Service when the Secretary of the Interior determines that the visitor uses a specific or specialized facility, equipment, or service.

children.label: i
children.text: Tent or trailer spaces.

children.label: ii
children.text: Picnic tables.

children.label: iii
children.text: Drinking water.

children.label: iv
children.text: Access roads.

children.label: v
children.text: The collection of the fee by an employee or agent of the Federal land management agency.

children.label: vi
children.text: Reasonable visitor protection.

children.label: vii
children.text: Refuse containers.

children.label: viii
children.text: Toilet facilities.

children.label: ix
children.text: Simple devices for containing a campfire.

children.label: B
children.text: Use of highly developed boat launches with specialized facilities or services such as mechanical or hydraulic boat lifts or facilities, multi-lane paved ramps, paved parking, restrooms and other improvements such as boarding floats, loading ramps, or fish cleaning stations.

children.label: C
children.text: Rental of cabins, boats, stock animals, lookouts, historic structures, group day-use or overnight sites, audio tour devices, portable sanitation devices, binoculars or other equipment.

children.label: D
children.text: Use of hookups for electricity, cable, or sewer.

children.label: E
children.text: Use of sanitary dump stations.

children.label: F
children.text: Participation in an enhanced interpretive program or special tour.

children.label: G
children.text: Use of reservation services.

children.label: H
children.text: Use of transportation services.

children.label: I
children.text: Use of areas where emergency medical or first-aid services are administered from facilities staffed by public employees or employees under a contract or reciprocal agreement with the Federal Government.

children.label: i
children.text: Bathhouse with showers and flush toilets.

children.label: ii
children.text: Refuse containers.

children.label: iii
children.text: Picnic areas.

children.label: iv
children.text: Paved parking.

children.label: v
children.text: Attendants, including lifeguards.

children.label: vi
children.text: Floats encompassing the swimming area.

children.label: vii
children.text: Swimming deck.

children.heading: Special recreation permits and fees
children.label: h Special recreation permits and fees
i
children.text: may develop and make available to the public an application to obtain a special recreation permit described in clause (i) of ; andsection 6801(13)(A) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: shall develop and make available to the public an application to obtain a special recreation permit described in each of clauses (ii) through (iv) of .section 6801(13)(A) of this title

children.heading: Issuance of permits
children.label: B Issuance of permits
children.text: On review of a completed application developed under subparagraph (A), as applicable, and a determination by the Secretary that the applicant is eligible for the special recreation permit, the Secretary may issue to the applicant a special recreation permit, subject to any terms and conditions that are determined to be necessary by the Secretary.

children.heading: Incidental sales
children.label: C Incidental sales
children.text: A special recreation permit issued under this paragraph may include an authorization for sales that are incidental in nature to the permitted use of the Federal recreational lands and waters, except where otherwise prohibited by law.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary may charge a special recreation permit fee for the issuance of a special recreation permit in accordance with this paragraph.

children.heading: Predetermined special recreation permit fees
children.label: B Predetermined special recreation permit fees

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: For purposes of subparagraphs (D) and (E) of this paragraph, the Secretary shall establish and may charge a predetermined fee, described in clause (ii) of this subparagraph, for a special recreation permit described in clause (iii) or (iv) of for a specific type of use on a unit of Federal recreational lands and waters, consistent with the criteria set forth in clause (iii) of this subparagraph.section 6801(13)(A) of this title

children.label: I
children.text: a fixed fee that is assessed per special recreation permit, including a fee with an associated size limitation or other criteria as determined to be appropriate by the Secretary; or

children.label: II
children.text: an amount assessed per visitor-use day.

children.label: I
children.text: have been established before ;January 4, 2025

children.label: II
children.text: be established after , in accordance with subsection (b);January 4, 2025

children.label: aa
children.text: be established after ; andJanuary 4, 2025

children.label: bb
children.text: be comparable to an amount described in subparagraph (D)(ii) or (E)(ii), as applicable; or

children.label: IV
children.text: beginning on the date that is 2 years after , be $6 per visitor-use day in instances in which the Secretary has not established a predetermined fee under subclause (I), (II), or (III).January 4, 2025

children.heading: Calculation of fees for specialized recreational uses and large-group activities or events
children.label: C Calculation of fees for specialized recreational uses and large-group activities or events
children.text: The Secretary may, at the discretion of the Secretary, establish and charge a fee for a special recreation permit described in clause (i) or (ii) of .section 6801(13)(A) of this title

children.heading: Calculation of fees for single organized group recreation activities or events, competitive events, and certain recurring organized group recreation activities
children.label: D Calculation of fees for single organized group recreation activities or events, competitive events, and certain recurring organized group recreation activities
i
children.text: the applicable predetermined fee established under subparagraph (B); or

children.label: ii
children.text: an amount equal to a percentage of, to be determined by the Secretary, but to not to exceed 5 percent of, adjusted gross receipts calculated under subparagraph (F).

children.heading: Calculation of fees for temporary permits and long-term permits
children.label: E Calculation of fees for temporary permits and long-term permits
i
children.text: the applicable predetermined fee established under subparagraph (B); or

children.label: ii
children.text: an amount equal to a percentage of, to be determined by the Secretary, but not to exceed 3 percent of, adjusted gross receipts calculated under subparagraph (F).

children.label: aa
children.text: the general amount paid by participants of the trip or event to the recreation service provider for the applicable trip or event (excluding amounts related to goods, souvenirs, merchandise, gear, and additional food provided or sold by the recreation service provider); and

children.label: AA
children.text: the number of days of the trip or event that occurred on Federal recreational lands and waters covered by the special recreation permit, rounded to the nearest whole day; by

children.label: BB
children.text: the total number of days of the trip or event; and

children.label: II
children.text: the amount of any additional revenue received by the recreation service provider for an add-on activity or an optional excursion that occurred on the Federal recreational lands and waters covered by the special recreation permit.

children.label: I
children.text: the total cost paid by the participants of the trip or event for the trip or event to the recreation service provider, including any additional revenue received by the recreation service provider for an add-on activity or an optional excursion that occurred on the Federal recreational lands and waters covered by the special recreation permit; and

children.label: aa
children.text: the amount of any revenues from goods, souvenirs, merchandise, gear, and additional food provided or sold by the recreation service provider to the participants of the applicable trip or event;

children.label: bb
children.text: the amount of any costs or revenues from services and activities provided or sold by the recreation service provider to the participants of the trip or event that occurred in a location other than the Federal recreational lands and waters covered by the special recreation permit (including costs for travel and lodging outside the Federal recreational lands and waters covered by the special recreation permit); and

children.label: cc
children.text: the amount of any revenues from any service provided by a recreation service provider for an activity on Federal recreational lands and waters that is not covered by the special recreation permit.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: G Exception
children.text: Notwithstanding subparagraph (E), the Secretary may charge a recreation service provider a minimum annual fee for a special recreation permit described in .section 6801(13)(A)(iv) of this title

children.label: I
children.text: a concession contract administered by the National Park Service or the United States Fish and Wildlife Service for the provision of accommodations, facilities, or services; or

children.label: II
children.text: a commercial use authorization or special use permit for use of Federal recreational lands and waters managed by the National Park Service.

children.heading: Cost recovery
children.label: ii Cost recovery
children.text: Nothing in this paragraph affects the ability of the Secretary to recover any administrative costs under .section 8549 of this title

children.heading: Special recreation permit fees and other recreation fees
children.label: iii Special recreation permit fees and other recreation fees
children.text: The collection of a special recreation permit fee under this paragraph shall not affect the authority of the Secretary to collect an entrance fee, a standard amenity recreation fee, or an expanded amenity recreation fee authorized under subsections (e), (f), and (g).

children.heading: Disclosure of recreation fees and use of recreation fees
children.label: i Disclosure of recreation fees and use of recreation fees

children.heading: Notice of entrance fees, standard amenity recreation fees, expanded amenity recreation fees, and available recreation passes
children.label: 1 Notice of entrance fees, standard amenity recreation fees, expanded amenity recreation fees, and available recreation passes
i
children.text: at appropriate locations in each unit or area of Federal recreational land and waters at which an entrance fee, standard amenity recreation fee, or expanded amenity recreation fee is charged; and

children.label: ii
children.text: on the appropriate website for such unit or area.

children.heading: Publications
children.label: B Publications
children.text: The Secretary shall include in publications distributed at a unit or area or described in subparagraph (A) the notice described in that subparagraph.

children.heading: Notice of uses of recreation fees
children.label: 2 Notice of uses of recreation fees
A
children.text: the total recreation fees collected during each of the 2 preceding fiscal years at the respective unit or area of the Federal land management agency; and

children.label: B
children.text: each use during the preceding fiscal year of the applicable recreation fee or recreation pass revenues collected under this section.

children.heading: Notice of recreation fee projects
children.label: 3 Notice of recreation fee projects
children.text: To the extent practicable, the Secretary shall post clear notice at the location at which work is performed using recreation fee and recreation pass revenues collected under this section.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than , and not later than 60 days after the beginning of each fiscal year thereafter, the Secretary shall post on the website of the applicable Federal land management agency a searchable list of each use during the preceding fiscal year of the recreation fee or recreation pass revenues collected under this section.January 1, 2025

children.label: i
children.text: a title and description of the overall project;

children.label: ii
children.text: a title and description for each component of the project;

children.label: iii
children.text: the location of the project; and

children.label: iv
children.text: the amount obligated for the project.

children.heading: Notice to customers
children.label: 5 Notice to customers
children.text: A recreation service provider may inform a customer of the recreation service provider of any fee charged by the Secretary under this section.

children.label: A
children.text: entrance fees under subsection (e);

children.label: B
children.text: standard amenity recreation fees under subsection (f);

children.label: C
children.text: expanded amenity recreation fees under subsection (g); and

children.label: D
children.text: special recreation permit fees.

children.heading: Distribution of online payments
children.label: 2 Distribution of online payments
children.text: An online payment collected under paragraph (1) that is associated with a specific unit or area of a Federal land management agency shall be distributed in accordance with .section 6804(c) of this title
